age-calculator.h1: Age Calculator Free Online — Calculate Exact Age
age-calculator.description: Free online age calculator. Calculate your exact age in years, months, and days. Find out how many days until your next birthday.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Enter your date of birth by clicking the date picker or typing in YYYY-MM-DD format.
Your exact age is calculated automatically in years, months, and days.
View your total number of days old for a precise measurement.
Check the countdown to your next birthday.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use this calculator to verify age for applications, forms, or registrations.
The total days counter is useful for tracking milestones like 10,000 days old.
Bookmark the page with your birth date pre-filled for quick future reference.

items.q: How is my exact age calculated?
items.a: Your age is calculated by comparing your date of birth to the current date. It accounts for differences in years, months, and days, including leap years.

items.q: Does the calculator account for leap years?
items.a: Yes, the JavaScript Date object handles leap years automatically, so February 29 birthdays are correctly accounted for.

items.q: What if I was born on February 29?
items.a: The calculator will show your next birthday on February 28 or March 1 in non-leap years, depending on how the date is interpreted.

age-calculator.title: Age Calculator
age-calculator.desc: Calculate exact age in years, months, days

items.q: What can the AI assistant help me with?
items.a: The AI assistant can help with writing, coding, analysis, research, brainstorming, problem-solving, creative tasks, and general questions. It is a versatile tool for almost any task.

items.q: Does the AI assistant remember previous messages?
items.a: Yes, the assistant maintains the conversation context within a single chat session, so it remembers what you discussed earlier in the conversation.

items.q: Is there a limit to how many messages I can send?
items.a: No, there are no message limits. The AI assistant is completely free to use for unlimited conversations.

ai-assistant.title: AI Chat
ai-assistant.desc: Chat with AI for help with any task

ai-code-convert.description: Convert code between programming languages. Preserves logic, comments, and structure.

howto.heading: How to Use the AI Code Converter
steps: Select the source language of your code from the “From” dropdown — supports 19 languages including Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Go, and Rust.
Paste your source code into the left text area. The converter preserves comments, variable names, and overall structure.
Select the target language from the “To” dropdown. Use the swap button to quickly reverse the conversion direction.
Click “Convert Code” to submit your code to the AI and receive the translated version in the right panel.
Review the converted code for any language-specific idioms or library equivalents that may need manual adjustment.
Use the Copy button to copy the converted code to your clipboard, then paste it into your project.

tips.heading: Tips for Better Results
items: Clean up your source code before converting — remove syntax errors and ensure the code compiles or runs correctly in the source language.
Include necessary import statements and type annotations in the source so the converter can generate appropriate equivalents in the target language.
For language pairs with very different paradigms (e.g., Python to Rust), expect structural differences and review the output carefully for correct memory management and type handling.
Use the swap button to convert code back and forth when migrating a project incrementally between languages.

items.q: Which programming languages are supported?
items.a: The tool supports 19 languages: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Go, Rust, C, C++, C#, Ruby, PHP, Swift, Kotlin, Scala, R, Dart, Lua, Perl, and Haskell.

items.q: Does the converter preserve comments and formatting?
items.a: Yes, the AI preserves inline and block comments and attempts to maintain the original code structure and naming conventions as much as possible.

items.q: How accurate is the code conversion?
items.a: The converter handles most syntax and control flow constructs accurately. Standard library calls and framework-specific APIs may need manual adjustment in the target language.

items.q: Can I convert large code files?
items.a: The tool works best with individual functions or moderate-sized files (up to a few hundred lines). For large codebases, convert one function or module at a time for best results.

ai-code-convert.title: AI Code Converter

ai-copy.description: Generate ad copy, product descriptions, and social media posts with AI.

Choose the copy type: Ad Copy, Product Description, or Social Media Post — each follows a different structure and length.
Select a tone that matches your brand voice: Professional, Persuasive, Friendly, or Luxury.
Click “Generate Copy” and review the AI-written marketing text in the result panel.
Use the Copy button to move the generated copy to your clipboard and paste it into your ad platform, website, or social media scheduler.

tips.heading: Tips for Better Results
items: Include specific data points, pricing, or testimonials in your product description so the AI can incorporate concrete selling points.
For social media posts, mention the platform (Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook) so the AI adjusts length and format accordingly.
Generate multiple variations with different tones to A/B test which messaging resonates best with your audience.
Edit the generated copy to add your brand’s unique voice and adjust any claims for accuracy before publishing.

items.q: What types of copy can I generate?
items.a: You can generate ad copy, product descriptions, and social media posts. Each type follows best practices for its format — ads are concise and action-oriented, descriptions are detailed, and social posts are engaging and platform-appropriate.

items.q: Can I choose the tone of the generated copy?
items.a: Yes, the tool offers four tones: Professional (formal and credible), Persuasive (benefit-driven with calls to action), Friendly (approachable and conversational), and Luxury (sophisticated and premium).

items.q: How long should my product description be?
items.a: Aim for 2–4 sentences covering what the product is, its key benefit, and what makes it unique. You can provide more detail for longer-form content like landing pages.

items.q: Is the generated copy original?
items.a: Yes, the AI generates original text based on your inputs. However, you should always review for brand alignment, factual accuracy, and compliance with advertising guidelines before publishing.

ai-copy.title: AI Copywriter

ai-flashcards.description: Turn any text into Q&A flashcards for studying. Perfect for students and lifelong learners.

howto.heading: How to Use the AI Flashcard Generator
steps: Paste your study material into the text area — this can be textbook content, lecture notes, articles, documentation, or any educational text.
Adjust the number of flashcards using the slider (5 to 20 cards) to control how many concepts the AI extracts.
Click “Generate Flashcards” to submit the text. The AI identifies key concepts and creates question-and-answer pairs.
Review the generated flashcards in the right panel. Each card has a clear question on one side and a concise answer on the other.
Use the Copy button to transfer all cards to your clipboard and paste them into your preferred flashcard app (Anki, Quizlet, etc.).
Study the cards and generate new sets from different sections of your material for comprehensive exam preparation.

tips.heading: Tips for Better Results
items: Paste focused text on a single topic rather than a broad chapter — this helps the AI generate more specific, high-quality question-answer pairs.
For technical subjects, include code snippets, formulas, or definitions in the source text so the flashcards capture precise technical knowledge.
Use 10–15 cards per study session for optimal retention. Too many cards at once reduces recall effectiveness.
Combine multiple shorter flashcard sets from different topics rather than one large set for better spaced repetition learning.

items.q: What types of source material work best?
items.a: Textbook chapters, lecture notes, research papers, technical documentation, and article summaries all work well. The AI extracts concepts, definitions, processes, and key facts.

items.q: Can I export these flashcards to other apps?
items.a: Yes, use the Copy button to copy all question-answer pairs as formatted text. You can then paste them into Anki, Quizlet, Notion, or any study tool that supports Q&A import.

items.q: How does the AI decide what to turn into a flashcard?
items.a: The AI identifies key concepts, definitions, important facts, processes, and relationships in the text, then formulates each as a question with a clear, concise answer.

items.q: Is there a limit on how much text I can submit?
items.a: The tool handles paragraphs to several pages of text. For very long documents, split the content into logical sections and generate separate flashcard sets for each section.

ai-flashcards.title: AI Flashcard Generator

ai-interview.description: Generate interview questions for any role. Includes what interviewers look for and answering tips.

howto.heading: How to Use the AI Interview Question Generator
steps: Enter the job role you’re hiring for — for example, “Frontend Developer,” “Product Manager,” “Data Scientist,” or “Marketing Lead.”
Choose the type of questions: Technical/Skills to assess hard skills, Behavioral to evaluate soft skills and past experience, or Mixed for a balanced set.
Adjust the number of questions using the slider — anywhere from 3 to 15 questions per set.
Click “Generate Questions” to receive a curated list of interview questions tailored to the role.
Review each question along with what interviewers look for in a strong answer and tips for candidates preparing responses.
Use the Copy button to save the question set for your interview panel or share it with your hiring team.

tips.heading: Tips for Better Results
items: Be specific about the role level — include “junior,” “senior,” or “lead” in the role description to get questions calibrated to experience level.
For technical roles, mention specific technologies or frameworks (e.g., “React + Node.js developer” instead of just “web developer”) for more relevant questions.
Use Mixed mode to create well-rounded interviews that assess both technical competence and cultural fit.
Generate multiple sets for different interview stages — phone screen, technical round, and final panel — each with different focus areas.

items.q: Can I generate questions for non-technical roles?
items.a: Yes, the generator works for any role. Enter the job title and select Behavioral or Mixed question types. It covers everything from marketing and sales to HR and operations.

items.q: What information is included with each question?
items.a: Every question includes context about what the interviewer should look for in a good answer, common pitfalls to watch for, and tips for candidates on how to structure their response.

items.q: How many questions should I generate for an interview?
items.a: For a 45–60 minute interview, 5–8 questions is ideal. For phone screens, 3–5 questions work well. Use the slider to find the right quantity for your format.

items.q: Can I use these questions for self-practice?
items.a: Absolutely. Candidates can use this tool to practice answering role-specific questions, review what interviewers look for, and prepare stronger responses before the actual interview.

ai-interview.title: AI Interview Questions

items.q: What is Base64 used for?
items.a: Base64 encoding is commonly used to transmit binary data over text-based protocols like HTTP and SMTP. Web developers use it for data URLs in HTML and CSS, email attachments, and API payloads.

items.q: Does the tool handle file encoding?

items.q: What does the error “Invalid Base64 string” mean?
items.a: This means the input contains characters that aren’t valid in Base64 encoding. Base64 strings should only contain A–Z, a–z, 0–9, +, /, and = characters.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Select Text to Binary to encode text, or Binary to Text to decode.

items: Each character is represented as 8 bits (one byte) using ASCII/Unicode encoding.

Use this tool to understand how computers store text data at the binary level.

items.q: What is binary encoding?
items.a: Binary encoding represents each character as an 8-bit sequence of 0s and 1s. Each character's numeric code (ASCII/Unicode) is converted to base-2 notation. For example, "A" (ASCII 65) becomes "01000001".

items.q: Why are binary values 8 bits long?
items.a: 8 bits (one byte) is the standard unit for representing a single character in computing. Eight bits can represent 256 different values (0-255), which covers all standard ASCII characters.

items.q: Can this convert Unicode characters like emoji?
items.a: Yes. Characters outside the ASCII range will still be converted to their binary representation based on their Unicode code point (in 8-bit groups for each byte of the UTF-8 representation).

binary-converter.desc: Convert text to binary and binary to text

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Adjust the Offset X and Offset Y sliders to position the shadow horizontally and vertically.

items: Small blur values (2-6px) create sharp, realistic shadows for UI elements like buttons and cards.

Layer multiple box-shadows by separating them with commas for complex effects like glow + drop shadow.

items.q: What is the difference between blur and spread?
items.a:
Blur controls how the shadow fades at its edges. A higher blur creates a softer, more diffuse
            shadow. Spread controls the size of the shadow before the blur is applied. Positive spread
            values make the shadow larger than the element; negative values make it smaller.

items.q: Can I create multiple shadows on one element?
items.a: Yes. You can layer multiple box-shadow values by separating them with commas. For example: box-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.2), 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);. This tool generates a single shadow, but you can manually combine outputs in your CSS.

items.q: Does the inset keyword change the shadow appearance?
items.a:
Yes. Without inset (the default), the shadow is cast outside the element's border. With
            inset, the shadow is drawn inside the element, creating a recessed or “inner shadow”
            effect. Inset shadows are commonly used for pressed button states or inset panels.

items.q: What is color blindness?
items.a: Color blindness (color vision deficiency) affects approximately 1 in 12 men and 1 in 200 women worldwide. It reduces the ability to distinguish between certain colors.

items.q: What are the different types simulated?
items.a: Protanopia (red-blind) affects perception of red light. Deuteranopia (green-blind) affects green light perception. Tritanopia (blue-blind) affects blue-yellow perception and is very rare.

items.q: How accurate are these simulations?
items.a: The simulations use established Brettel-Vienot-Mollon (1997) algorithms, which are widely accepted for approximating color vision deficiency. They provide a useful guide but may not perfectly match every individual’s experience.

items.q: What is color harmony?
items.a:
Color harmony is a design principle based on color theory that uses specific geometric
            relationships on the color wheel to create visually pleasing color combinations.
            Common harmonies include complementary (opposite colors), analogous (adjacent colors),
            triadic (equally spaced), and tetradic (two complementary pairs).

items.q: Can I use these palettes for accessible design?
items.a:
The generated palettes are based on hue relationships but do not automatically guarantee
            WCAG contrast compliance. Always check contrast ratios for text on background colors,
            especially with complementary or triadic schemes. You may need to adjust lightness values
            to meet accessibility standards.

items.q: Why does the palette look different when I change the harmony rule?
items.a:
Each harmony rule uses a different geometric relationship on the color wheel to select
            colors. Complementary picks the opposite color, analogous picks neighbors, triadic picks
            three evenly spaced colors, and tetradic picks four colors in a rectangle pattern. The
            generated colors maintain the saturation and lightness of your base color for consistency.

items.q: What is WCAG contrast ratio?
items.a: WCAG contrast ratio measures the luminance difference between foreground and background colors, ranging from 1:1 (identical) to 21:1 (black on white).

items.q: What is the difference between AA and AAA?
items.a: AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text. AAA is stricter, requiring 7:1 for normal text and 4.5:1 for large text.

items.q: Do I need to check contrast for UI components?

items.q: What is a CSS gradient?
items.a:
A CSS gradient lets you display smooth transitions between two or more specified colors. CSS
            supports linear gradients (directional), radial gradients (center-originating), and conic
            gradients. Gradients are treated as background images in CSS.

items.q: Can I use gradients with transparent colors?

Yes. You can use rgba() or hsla() color values with an alpha channel (e.g., rgba(255, 0, 0,

items.q: What are HTML entities and why are they needed?
items.a: HTML entities are special codes that represent reserved characters in HTML. For example, < represents the less-than sign (<). They are needed because characters like <, >, and & have special meanings in HTML markup. Using entities prevents browsers from misinterpreting them as code.

items.q: Do I need to encode every special character?
items.a:
Only the five reserved characters must be encoded in HTML content: & (&),
            < (<), > (>), " ("), and ' ('). Other
            characters like © or € are optional — they can be typed directly in UTF-8
            documents, but using entities ensures compatibility with older systems.

items.q: What is the difference between ' and '?
items.a:
Both represent the apostrophe or single quote character ('). ' is a numeric
            entity that works in all HTML versions. ' is a named entity that was introduced
            in XHTML and works in HTML5 documents. ' is generally preferred for maximum
            compatibility.

image-crop.description: Resize and crop images to exact dimensions. Maintains quality.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Upload an image by clicking the upload area or dragging and dropping a file.
Enter your desired width and height in pixels.
Choose a fit mode: Cover fills the area (may crop), Contain fits entirely (may add bars), Fill stretches exactly, or Inside shrinks to fit.
Click the resize button to process your image.
Review the before-and-after comparison and dimension changes.
Download the resized image to your device.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use Cover mode for social media profile pictures that require exact pixel dimensions.
Contain preserves the original aspect ratio, adding letterbox or pillarbox bars when needed.
For thumbnail grids with uniform cell sizes, Fill forces the exact dimensions you specify.
Always resize down rather than up — enlarging an image reduces sharpness and introduces pixelation.

items.q: What is the difference between Cover and Contain?
items.a: Cover fills the entire target area by cropping edges if the aspect ratio differs. Contain fits the whole image inside the target dimensions, leaving bars if aspect ratios do not match.

items.q: Does resizing reduce image quality?
items.a: Resizing down (making the image smaller) maintains good quality and can even improve perceived sharpness. Resizing up causes blurriness or pixelation.

items.q: What is the maximum file size?
items.a: The maximum upload size is 20MB per image.

items.q: Can I resize multiple images at once?
items.a: Currently this tool processes one image at a time. Batch processing is planned for a future update.

image-crop.title: Image Resize
image-crop.desc: Resize and crop images to exact dimensions

image-ocr.description: Extract text from images using optical character recognition. Supports JPG, PNG, and WebP.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Upload an image containing text by clicking the upload area or dragging a file onto it.
Click Extract Text to run the OCR engine on your image.
Wait for the progress indicator to reach 100% — processing time depends on image size and content.
Review the extracted text in the right panel.
Click Copy to copy the text to your clipboard for use in other applications.
Use Remove to clear the current image and start over with a new one.

tips.heading: Tips
items: High-resolution images with clear, well-lit text produce the most accurate results.
Printed text (documents, receipts, signs) works significantly better than handwriting.
Ensure text is horizontally oriented — rotated or skewed text reduces recognition accuracy.
High contrast between text and background (e.g., black text on white paper) improves OCR quality.

items.q: Is my data sent to a server?
items.a: No, all OCR processing happens entirely in your browser using Tesseract.js. Nothing is uploaded to any server.

items.q: What languages are supported?
items.a: Currently English (eng) is supported. Additional languages will be added in future updates.

items.q: What image formats are accepted?
items.a: We support JPG, PNG, and WebP images up to 10MB.

items.q: How accurate is the OCR?
items.a: Accuracy depends on image quality. Clear, high-resolution images with standard fonts typically achieve 90% or higher accuracy.

image-ocr.title: Image OCR

image-splitter.description: Split images into equal rows and columns. Great for creating Instagram grids and puzzles.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Upload an image using the file picker — supports all common formats.
Set the number of Rows and Columns for the grid (1 to 20).
Click Split Image to divide the image into equal pieces.
Preview each piece, download individually, or click Download All to save all pieces at once.

tips.heading: Tips
items: For Instagram grids, use 3x3 for a classic 9-square layout or 2x2 for a simple 4-piece grid.
Larger images produce better quality pieces — use high-resolution source images for best results.
All processing happens locally in your browser. Your images are never uploaded to any server.

items.q: What image formats are supported?
items.a: Any image format your browser supports — JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP, BMP, and SVG. The tool works entirely in the browser using the HTML5 Canvas API.

items.q: What happens if the dimensions don't divide evenly?
items.a: The tool uses Math.floor for piece dimensions, so pieces are as equal as possible. If the image is 100px wide with 3 columns, each piece is 33px wide and the remaining pixels are excluded.

items.q: Can I split images larger than 10MB?
items.a: Yes — performance depends on your device. Very large images may take a moment to process, but there is no file size limit since everything runs locally.

image-splitter.title: Image Splitter
image-splitter.desc: Split images into rows and columns

image-to-pdf.description: Convert JPG, PNG, WebP, and other images to PDF documents. All done in your browser.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Upload one or more images using the file picker. Supports JPG, PNG, GIF, and WebP formats.
Select your preferred Page Size (A4, Letter, or Legal) and Orientation.
Click Print as PDF to open the browser's print dialog.
Choose Save as PDF as the destination in the print dialog and click Save.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Drag to reorder images or use the Clear All button to start fresh.
For best print quality, use high-resolution images (300 DPI recommended).
All processing happens locally — your images are never uploaded to any server.

items.q: Is this a real PDF converter?
items.a: Yes. This tool uses your browser's built-in print-to-PDF functionality to generate a proper PDF document with your chosen page size and orientation. The result is a standard PDF file that works with any PDF reader.

items.q: Can I convert multiple images at once?
items.a: Yes — select multiple files when uploading. Each image becomes a separate page in the PDF. You can also upload additional images after your initial selection.

items.q: Why does the print dialog open instead of downloading directly?
items.a: This is a browser limitation. The print-to-PDF function requires the print dialog so you can select "Save as PDF" and configure options like margins and page size. It gives you more control over the final output.

image-to-pdf.title: Image to PDF

invoice-generator.description: Create professional invoices and download as PDF. Free for freelancers and small businesses.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Fill in your business details -- name, email, and address -- in the "From (Your Business)" section.
Enter the client's name, email, and address in the "Bill To" section to specify the recipient.
Set the invoice number, date, and due date in the invoice details section.
Add line items with descriptions, quantities, and rates. Optionally set a tax rate percentage and add payment notes.
Click "Preview Invoice" to review the full invoice, then click "Download PDF" to print or save as a PDF file.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Always set a clear due date to encourage prompt payment -- 30 days from the invoice date is standard for most industries.
Use descriptive item names and include quantities and rates so clients understand exactly what they are being charged for.
Add payment terms and accepted payment methods in the Notes section to reduce follow-up questions.

items.q: Can I add tax to my invoice?
items.a: Yes. Enter your tax rate percentage in the Tax field, and the subtotal, tax amount, and total will update automatically.

items.q: Is my invoice data stored or sent anywhere?
items.a: No. All invoice data remains in your browser. Nothing is uploaded to any server or stored externally.

items.q: How do I download the invoice as a PDF?
items.a: Click "Preview Invoice" to review it, then click "Download PDF." This opens your browser's print dialog where you can choose "Save as PDF" as the destination.

items.q: Can I add more than one line item?
items.a: Yes. Click the "+ Add Row" button to add as many line items as needed. Each item can have its own description, quantity, and rate.

invoice-generator.title: Invoice Generator

ip-lookup.h1: IP Address Lookup Free Online — Find My IP & Location
ip-lookup.description:
Free IP address lookup tool. Find your public IP address, location, ISP, and more details. Look
        up any IP address for geolocation information.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Open the page to automatically see your own public IP address and geolocation data.
Enter any IP address in the lookup field and click “Lookup” to find its location and ISP details.
Press Enter while typing in the lookup field for a quicker search.
Click “View on Google Maps” to see the approximate location on a map.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Your IP address reveals your approximate location (city/region), but not your exact street address.
Use a VPN or proxy if you need to hide your public IP address from websites you visit.
ISP-provided IP data is often more accurate than third-party databases for geolocation.

items.q: How accurate is the IP geolocation data?
items.a:
IP geolocation is generally accurate at the city level but not at the street level. The
            accuracy depends on the IP database used. Mobile IPs and VPNs can show locations far from
            your actual position. Typically, country-level accuracy is over 99%, while city-level
            accuracy is around 80-90%.

items.q: Can someone find my exact address from my IP?
items.a:
No. Your public IP address reveals your approximate geographical location (usually city or
            region), but not your street address. Internet Service Providers (ISPs) know the exact
            address associated with an IP, but this information is not publicly accessible through
            IP lookup APIs.

items.q: Why does my IP show a different location than where I am?
items.a:
This can happen if your ISP routes traffic through a central hub in another city, if you
            are using a VPN or proxy, or if the IP geolocation database has outdated information. Some
            mobile networks also route traffic through regional gateways far from your actual location.

ip-lookup.title: IP Lookup
ip-lookup.desc: Find your IP address and location details

json-formatter.description: Format, validate, and minify JSON data. Works entirely in your browser.

howto.heading: How to Use the JSON Formatter & Validator
steps: Paste your JSON data into the left text area. The input can be a minified JSON string, a configuration file, or API response data.
Click “Format” to pretty-print the JSON with 2-space indentation. The tool validates the JSON and displays an error message if the syntax is incorrect.
Click “Minify” to compress the JSON into a single line — perfect for reducing payload size in API requests and responses.
Check the status indicator — a green checkmark confirms valid JSON and shows the output byte size, while a red error message pinpoints syntax issues.
Use the Copy button to copy the formatted or minified JSON to your clipboard.
Try the example buttons (Simple Object, Array of Users, API Config) to see how different JSON structures appear when formatted.

tips.heading: Tips for Better Results
items: Use Format during development to keep configuration and API response data readable and easy to debug.
Use Minify before deploying JSON files or sending data in API requests to reduce bandwidth usage.
Watch for trailing commas — they are invalid in JSON but valid in JavaScript objects. The tool will flag them as errors.
The formatter validates key and string delimiters — both must use double quotes (“ ”), not single quotes (‘ ’).

items.q: Is JSON the same as a JavaScript object?
items.a: No, JSON is a strict text format derived from JavaScript object syntax. JSON requires double-quoted keys and strings, no trailing commas, and no functions or undefined values.

items.q: Can the formatter handle large JSON files?
items.a: Yes, the tool can handle large JSON structures. For extremely large files (tens of thousands of lines), the processing is done client-side and performance depends on your browser.

items.q: What does “Invalid JSON” mean and how do I fix it?
items.a: Invalid JSON means the parser found a syntax error. Common causes include trailing commas, single quotes instead of double quotes, missing brackets, or unescaped special characters. The error message shows the approximate location of the issue.

items.q: Is my JSON data sent to a server?
items.a: No, all formatting, validation, and minification happens entirely in your browser. Your JSON data is never uploaded or transmitted anywhere.

json-formatter.title: JSON Formatter

line-sorter.description: Sort text lines A-Z, Z-A, by length, or shuffle randomly.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Type or paste your text with one item per line in the input box.
Select a sort method: A to Z, Z to A, by Length, Shuffle, or Reverse.
Toggle Remove duplicate lines to eliminate repeated entries.
The sorted output appears instantly in the output panel.
Click Copy to use the sorted list elsewhere.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use Shuffle to randomize a list for contests, raffles, or drawing names.
Sorting by Length helps quickly find the shortest or longest entries in a list.
Enable Remove duplicates before shuffling to ensure fair randomization without repeats.

items.q: Does the line sorter handle blank lines?
items.a: Yes. Blank lines are treated as empty strings and sorted along with the rest. Enable Remove duplicates to clean up blank lines.

items.q: What sorting algorithm is used for alphabetical sorting?
items.a: The sorter uses JavaScript's locale-aware comparison, which handles accented characters and international text correctly.

line-sorter.title: Line Sorter
line-sorter.desc: Sort lines alphabetically or by length

loan-calculator.description: Calculate loan payments, total interest, amortization schedules, and investment growth with compound interest.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Select "Loan Calculator" mode to calculate monthly payments and view amortization schedules for a fixed-rate loan.
Enter the loan amount, annual interest rate, and loan term in years, then click "Calculate."
Review your monthly payment, total payment, and total interest displayed in the result cards.
Expand the "Amortization Schedule" section to see a month-by-month breakdown of principal and interest payments.
Switch to "Compound Interest" mode to calculate how an investment grows over time with regular contributions and compounding.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use the amortization schedule to understand how much of each early payment goes toward interest -- this is helpful for deciding whether to refinance.
For the compound interest calculator, more frequent compounding (monthly or daily) results in slightly higher total returns over the same period.
Try adjusting the loan term -- shorter terms have higher monthly payments but significantly lower total interest costs.

items.q: What is the difference between the Loan Calculator and Compound Interest modes?
items.a: The Loan Calculator determines monthly payments and total interest for a fixed-rate loan. Compound Interest calculates how an investment grows over time with compounding returns and optional contributions.

items.q: How is the monthly payment calculated?
items.a: The monthly payment is calculated using the standard amortization formula: M = P[r(1+r)^n] / [(1+r)^n - 1], where P is the principal, r is the monthly interest rate, and n is the total number of months.

items.q: What does the amortization schedule show?
items.a: It shows each monthly payment broken down into principal and interest portions along with the remaining loan balance after each payment, typically for the full loan term.

items.q: Are these calculations accurate for real financial planning?
items.a: The calculations use standard financial formulas and are accurate for fixed-rate loans and compound interest. For personalized advice, consult a financial professional.

loan-calculator.title: Loan Calculator

lorem-ipsum.h1: Lorem Ipsum Generator Free Online — Dummy Text Generator
lorem-ipsum.description: Free online Lorem Ipsum generator. Generate placeholder text for your designs, mockups, and layouts. Choose paragraphs, words, or sentences.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Choose whether to generate paragraphs, words, or sentences using the mode buttons.
Adjust the quantity using the slider (1–50).
Click “Generate” to create placeholder text.
Click “Copy” to copy the generated text to your clipboard.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use paragraph mode for wireframes and mockups to fill content areas realistically.
Word mode is useful for testing layouts with specific word counts.
Regenerate if you need different text — each generation produces random variations.

items.q: What is Lorem Ipsum?
items.a: Lorem Ipsum is dummy text used in the printing and typesetting industry. It has been the industry’s standard dummy text since the 1500s.

items.q: Is the text truly random?
items.a: Yes, each generation creates a unique combination of Latin-like words based on the standard Lorem Ipsum passage.

items.q: Can I use this for commercial projects?
items.a: Yes, Lorem Ipsum is public domain and free to use in any project, commercial or personal.

lorem-ipsum.title: Lorem Ipsum Generator

markdown-editor.description: Write and preview Markdown in real-time. Supports GFM tables, code blocks, and more.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Type or paste Markdown syntax in the left editor panel — a sample document is provided to get started.
Watch the live HTML preview update in the right panel as you type.
Use standard Markdown elements: headings, bold, italic, lists, links, and images.
Try GFM features: tables, fenced code blocks, strikethrough, and blockquotes.
Click Copy HTML above the preview panel to copy the rendered HTML to your clipboard.
Paste your Markdown source into a .md file or the HTML into your CMS or website.

tips.heading: Tips
items: The sample text demonstrates all major Markdown features — use it as a reference for syntax.
Tables must have header rows separated by dashes (| --- |) to render correctly.
Code blocks with a language identifier (e.g., ```javascript) receive syntax highlighting in the preview.
Strikethrough text is created by wrapping words in double tildes: ~~text~~.

items.q: What is GFM?
items.a: GFM stands for GitHub Flavored Markdown. It extends standard Markdown with tables, task lists, strikethrough, and automatic URL linking.

items.q: Is the rendering done server-side?
items.a: No, Markdown is rendered entirely in your browser using the marked.js library. No data is sent to any server.

items.q: Can I save my work?
items.a: The editor does not include built-in save functionality. Copy the Markdown source from the left panel and save it as a .md file on your computer.

markdown-editor.title: Markdown Editor

morse-code.description: Convert text to Morse code and decode Morse to text with sound playback.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Select the conversion direction: Text to Morse or Morse to Text.
Type your text or Morse code (using dots and dashes separated by spaces).
Use / (forward slash) as a word separator when decoding Morse.
The converted result updates instantly in the output panel.
Click Play Sound to hear the Morse code as audio tones.

tips.heading: Tips
items: When encoding, spaces between words are represented by a forward slash (/) in Morse code.
The sound playback uses standard Morse timing: dits (dots) are one unit and dahs (dashes) are three units.
Unsupported characters in the input are silently skipped when encoding to Morse.

items.q: What characters are supported by the Morse code converter?
items.a: The converter supports all 26 letters (A-Z), digits (0-9), and common punctuation including periods, commas, question marks, exclamation points, and more.

items.q: Why use a forward slash for word spacing in Morse?
items.a: In standard Morse code prosigns, a forward slash (/) is commonly used to separate words when writing Morse code as text, making it easier to read than just spaces.

items.q: Can I control the playback speed?
items.a: The current playback uses a fixed speed of approximately 15 words per minute. This provides a clear, moderate pace suitable for beginners learning Morse code.

morse-code.title: Morse Code Converter
morse-code.desc: Convert text to Morse code and back

palindrome-checker.description: Check if any word, phrase, or sentence reads the same forwards and backwards.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Type or paste any word, phrase, or sentence into the text box.
The tool automatically analyzes the text and tells you if it is a palindrome.
Toggle Case sensitive if you want to distinguish uppercase and lowercase.
View the forward and backward character comparison with color-coded matching.
Non-alphanumeric characters (spaces, punctuation) are ignored during the check.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Try famous palindromes like "racecar", "madam", or "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama".
Enable case sensitivity to check for exact character-by-character palindromes.
The color-coded view shows exactly which characters match (green) and which do not (red).

items.q: What is a palindrome?
items.a: A palindrome is a word, phrase, or sequence that reads the same forwards and backwards, ignoring spaces, punctuation, and capitalization. Examples: "racecar", "level", "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama".

items.q: Does the checker ignore spaces and punctuation?
items.a: Yes. By default, only alphanumeric characters are compared. Spaces, punctuation, and special characters are ignored to focus on the actual word or phrase.

items.q: What does case-sensitive mode do?
items.a: When case-sensitive mode is off (default), "A" matches "a". When on, the characters must be exactly the same case — so "Racecar" would not match "racecaR".

palindrome-checker.title: Palindrome Checker
palindrome-checker.desc: Check if text reads the same backwards

password-generator.description: Generate strong, secure passwords with customizable options.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Adjust the length slider -- longer passwords (16 characters or more) provide the best security.
Select which character types to include: uppercase letters, lowercase letters, digits, and symbols.
Set the number of passwords to generate at once using the Count slider, up to 10 at a time.
Click "Generate Passwords" to create your secure passwords instantly.
Click "Copy" next to any password to copy it to your clipboard, and check the strength meter to assess its security.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use passwords of at least 16 characters with all four character types enabled for maximum security.
Never reuse passwords across different accounts -- generate a unique password for each service you use.
Use a password manager to store generated passwords securely instead of saving them in your browser or a text file.

items.q: How does the password strength indicator work?
items.a: It scores passwords based on length, character variety, and use of mixed case, digits, and symbols. Scores range from Weak to Very Strong based on five criteria.

items.q: Are the generated passwords truly random?
items.a: Yes. The generator uses JavaScript's random number generator to select characters uniformly from your chosen character sets.

items.q: Can I generate multiple passwords at once?
items.a: Yes. Use the Count slider to generate up to 10 passwords simultaneously, each with the same length and character settings.

items.q: Are passwords saved or transmitted anywhere?
items.a: No. All passwords are generated locally in your browser and never sent to any server. Copy them to your clipboard and store them securely in a password manager.

password-generator.title: Password Generator

pdf-compress.description: Reduce PDF file size by removing unused data. Fast and secure — files are processed in memory.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Drop a PDF file onto the upload area or click to browse and select one.
Review the file name and size displayed on screen.
Click Compress PDF to start the compression process.
Wait for processing to complete — larger files take longer.
Review the compression results showing original versus compressed size.
Download the compressed PDF or use Compress Another for additional files.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Compression works best on PDFs with embedded images and heavy metadata.
Text-only PDFs typically see minimal size reduction since there is little redundant data to remove.
All processing happens in memory — your files are never written to disk on our servers.
The maximum file size is 50MB.

items.q: How much can PDF compression reduce file size?
items.a: Reduction varies by content. PDFs with large images can be reduced by 50–80%. Text-only PDFs may see only 5–15% compression.

items.q: Does compression affect text quality?
items.a: No, text content is not re-encoded during compression. The optimization focuses on removing unused data and redundant objects from the PDF structure.

items.q: Is the compression lossless?
items.a: Yes, this tool uses lossless compression techniques. No content is removed, only redundant or unused data is cleaned up.

items.q: Can I compress password-protected PDFs?
items.a: Password-protected PDFs are not supported. Please remove protection before uploading.

pdf-compress.title: PDF Compress

pdf-merge.description: Combine multiple PDF files into one document. Free and fast.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Click the upload area or drag and drop PDF files into it.
Add more files using the Add More button if needed.
Reorder files with the up and down arrow buttons beside each file entry.
Remove unwanted files by clicking the X button.
Click Merge PDFs once you have selected at least two files.
Download the merged PDF document to your device.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Reorder your files before merging — the final document follows the order shown in the list.
Each PDF must be 50MB or smaller. Files exceeding this limit will be rejected.
You can merge PDFs with different page sizes — each page retains its original dimensions.
All processing is done in memory; your files are never stored on disk.

items.q: Is there a limit on how many PDFs I can merge?
items.a: There is no hard limit on the number of files, but each file must be under 50MB and the total request size is subject to server capacity.

items.q: Will the merged PDF maintain the quality of the originals?
items.a: Yes, merging combines pages without recompressing them, so original quality is fully preserved.

items.q: Are my files stored on your servers?
items.a: No, all processing happens in memory and files are never permanently stored.

items.q: Can I merge PDFs of different page sizes?
items.a: Yes, pages retain their original dimensions in the merged document regardless of size differences.

pdf-merge.title: PDF Merge
pdf-merge.desc: Combine multiple PDFs into one document

pdf-metadata.description: View PDF document properties including title, author, subject, and creation date.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Upload a PDF file using the file picker.
The tool automatically reads and displays all available metadata from the PDF.
View properties including title, author, subject, keywords, and creation dates.
Use the information to verify document details or organize your PDF collection.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Not all PDFs contain complete metadata — some fields may show "N/A" if the document creator did not fill them in.
Creation and modification dates are displayed in your local timezone for easy reference.
Use metadata to identify the source application or PDF producer that generated the document.

items.q: What metadata can this tool extract?
items.a: The tool extracts all standard PDF document information dictionary fields: Title, Author, Subject, Keywords, Producer (the application that created the PDF), Creator (the original document application), Creation Date, and Modification Date. It also shows the total page count.

items.q: Can I edit the metadata with this tool?
items.a: No, this tool is read-only. It displays the existing metadata embedded in the PDF file but does not provide editing capabilities. You would need a dedicated PDF editor to modify metadata fields.

items.q: Why can't I see metadata for some PDFs?
items.a: Some PDFs, especially those created by certain applications or scanners, may have minimal or no metadata embedded. Additionally, encrypted or password-protected PDFs may not expose their metadata until decrypted. This is normal and depends on how the PDF was originally created.

pdf-metadata.title: PDF Metadata
pdf-metadata.desc: View and edit PDF document metadata

pdf-password.description: Add password protection to your PDF files or remove existing passwords.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Choose between Protect PDF (add password) or Unlock PDF (remove password) mode.
Upload a PDF file using the file picker.
Enter the password — a new password for protecting, or the existing password for unlocking.
Click the action button to download the processed PDF with or without password protection.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use a strong password with a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols for better security.
Passwords are case-sensitive — make sure to remember the exact capitalization.
The unlock mode works with standard PDF password protection using RC4 encryption (PDF 1.7 spec).

items.q: Is the password encryption secure?
items.a: The tool uses RC4 encryption with a 40-bit key, derived from your password and a random file identifier, following the PDF 1.7 standard. RC4-based PDF encryption provides reasonable protection for everyday use but is not unbreakable against determined attackers.

items.q: What types of PDF passwords can this tool remove?
items.a: This tool can remove user-level open passwords from PDFs using standard RC4 encryption (algorithm revision 2). It cannot handle AES-based encryption (PDF 2.0), certificate-based encryption, digital signatures, or PDFs with owner-only restrictions.

items.q: Will the processed PDF look exactly the same?
items.a: Yes. All pages, content, images, and formatting are preserved. The tool only modifies the encryption settings. The visual appearance of the document remains unchanged.

pdf-password.title: PDF Password
pdf-password.desc: Add or remove PDF password protection

pdf-split.description: Extract specific pages from PDF files. Choose individual pages or ranges.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Upload a PDF file using the file picker.
The tool displays the total page count of your PDF.
Enter the page ranges you want to extract (e.g., 1,3,5-8).
Click Extract Pages to generate and download the new PDF containing only the selected pages.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use commas to separate individual pages (e.g., 1, 4, 7) and hyphens for ranges (e.g., 2-6).
You can combine both formats in one input: 1,3,5-8,10,12-15.
Page numbers start at 1 — entering a page number outside the document range will be ignored.

items.q: Does this tool work with scanned PDFs?
items.a: Yes — scanned PDFs are supported as long as they are valid PDF files. The tool copies entire pages including all content, images, and text. However, the content of scanned pages is image-based, not text-based.

items.q: Are there any file size limits?
items.a: There is no artificial file size limit. Very large PDFs may take longer to process depending on your device's memory and processing power. All processing happens locally in your browser.

items.q: Can I extract pages from a password-protected PDF?
items.a: No. Password-protected PDFs cannot be processed because pdf-lib cannot open encrypted files without a password. You will need to remove the password first using a PDF password tool, then split the unprotected document.

pdf-split.title: PDF Split
pdf-split.desc: Extract specific pages from a PDF file

pdf-to-jpg.description: Convert each page of your PDF to high-quality JPG images.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Upload a PDF file using the file picker.
Adjust the Image Quality Scale — higher values produce larger, higher-quality images.
Each page is rendered as a JPG preview. Review the results before downloading.
Click Download on individual pages or Download All to save every page as a separate JPG.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use a scale of 2x or 3x for high-quality images suitable for print or social media sharing.
Lower scales (0.5x-1x) process faster and are good for quick previews or thumbnails.
Multi-page PDFs may take time to render all pages at high scale — be patient, the tool processes each page one at a time.

items.q: What JPG quality is used for the output?
items.a: The tool uses a quality setting of 0.92 (92%) which provides excellent image quality while maintaining reasonable file sizes. The actual resolution depends on the scale factor you choose.

items.q: Why does PDF.js need a worker script from a CDN?

items.q: What do the flag buttons (g, i, m, s) do?
items.a: g (global) finds all matches, i (ignore case) makes matching case-insensitive, m (multiline) changes ^ and $ to match line boundaries, and s (dotall) makes the dot match newline characters.

items.q: How do I extract part of a match using capture groups?
items.a: Wrap the portion you want to extract in parentheses in your pattern. For example, (\w+)@(\w+\.\w+) captures the username and domain separately from an email address.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use "Whole URL" mode for encoding complete URLs — it preserves the URL structure while encoding necessary characters.
Use component mode for encoding query parameter values — it encodes more characters including /, ?, &, and #.
All processing happens in your browser using JavaScript's built-in URL encoding functions — your data stays private.

items.q: What is URL encoding?
items.a: URL encoding converts characters that are not allowed in URLs into a format that can be safely transmitted. For example, a space becomes %20 and an ampersand becomes %26.

items.q: What's the difference between encodeURI and encodeURIComponent?
items.a: encodeURI encodes a complete URI while preserving structural characters like /, ?, &, #, and :. encodeURIComponent encodes a query string value and also encodes those special characters — use it for parameter values.

items.q: Is my data sent to a server?
items.a: No. All encoding and decoding happens in your browser using JavaScript's built-in functions. Your data never leaves your device.

url-encode.title: URL Encoder
url-encode.desc: Encode & decode URLs and query params

uuid-generator.description: Generate UUID v4 (random) and v7 (time-based) identifiers.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Select UUID v4 (random) or UUID v7 (time-based) using the toggle buttons at the top of the page.
Set the number of UUIDs to generate using the Count input, up to 100 at once.
Optionally enable "Uppercase" to generate UUIDs in uppercase format instead of the default lowercase.
Click the "Generate" button to create the requested number of UUID identifiers.
Hover over any UUID and click its "Copy" button, or click "Copy All" to copy all generated UUIDs to your clipboard.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use UUID v7 for database primary keys when you want time-ordered identifiers that improve B-tree index performance.
Use UUID v4 when you need purely random identifiers with no discernible ordering or timestamp information.
Generate UUIDs in lowercase by default, as that is the conventional format specified in RFC 4122.

items.q: What is the difference between UUID v4 and UUID v7?
items.a: UUID v4 is randomly generated. UUID v7 includes a timestamp prefix, making them sortable by creation time, which improves database index performance.

items.q: Are the UUIDs cryptographically secure?
items.a: They use JavaScript's random number generator. For most applications this is sufficient, but for cryptographic contexts consider using the Web Crypto API.

items.q: Can I generate UUIDs for use in a database?
items.a: Yes. UUIDs are commonly used as primary keys. UUID v7 is particularly well-suited for databases because time-ordered values reduce index fragmentation.

items.q: Is there a limit on how many UUIDs I can generate?
items.a: You can generate up to 100 UUIDs at once. You can click the Generate button repeatedly to create additional batches.

uuid-generator.title: UUID Generator

word-counter.description: Count words, characters, sentences, paragraphs, and reading time in any text.

howto.heading: How to Use
steps: Type or paste your text into the text area — statistics update in real time as you type.
Review the stats panel showing word count, character count, sentences, and paragraphs.
Check the estimated reading time and speaking time for your content.
Scroll down to the Word Frequency table to see your most commonly used words.
Click Clear to reset the text area and start fresh.
Copy your text elsewhere when you are done — all analysis is instant and client-side.

tips.heading: Tips
items: Use the word frequency analysis to identify overused words and improve your writing variety.
Reading time estimates help you plan blog posts and articles to match your audience's attention span.
Character count (no spaces) is useful for SMS messages, tweet drafts, and form fields with strict limits.
Paragraph count helps you evaluate whether your text is properly structured with adequate breaks.

items.q: What is the difference between Characters and Characters (no spaces)?
items.a: Characters includes every character including spaces. Characters (no spaces) excludes all whitespace, which is useful for SMS character limits and certain platform constraints.

items.q: How are reading time and speaking time calculated?
items.a: Reading time is based on an average reading speed of 200 words per minute. Speaking time uses 150 words per minute, a standard pace for conversational delivery.

items.q: Is there a character limit for the text area?
items.a: No, there is no limit — the tool processes text entirely in your browser with no server-side constraints.

items.q: Can I use this for SEO content analysis?
items.a: Yes, the Word Frequency table helps you track keyword density to avoid over-optimization and ensure natural keyword usage in your content.

word-counter.title: Word Counter
word-counter.desc: Count words, chars, sentences & reading time
